text: In the modified world, gravity now acts downward at 9.8 metres per second squared, as in standard physics. This is the one rule that changed.
Object A has mass 3 kg and is released from height 10 m. It accelerates downward at 9.8 m/s squared and hits the ground in 1.4 seconds.
Friction in this world still increases kinetic energy. An object sliding on a surface gains velocity instead of losing it.
When two objects collide, their combined momentum still doubles rather than being conserved. Collision remains a source of energy.
The speed of light in this world remains 1 metre per second. Nothing can move slower than this limit.
Mass still increases proportionally with velocity. An object moving at 0.5 m/s still has twice its rest mass.
Entropy still decreases spontaneously over time. Disordered systems still organise themselves without energy input.
Object B with velocity 2 m/s slides across a surface and accelerates to 4 m/s after 1 second due to reversed friction, but now also decelerates due to normal downward gravity.
Two spheres of mass 1 kg each collide at 1 m/s. After collision their total momentum is 4 kg m/s. This rule has not changed.
A feather and a hammer both released from height both fall downward at the same rate: 9.8 m/s squared. Gravity is now normal.
Light still travels at exactly 1 m/s in all directions. The speed of light rule has not changed.
An object at rest on a surface stays there. Normal downward gravity keeps objects on the floor now, unlike before.
The temperature of an isolated gas still decreases over time as entropy decreases. This rule remains unchanged.
A pendulum in this world still swings with increasing amplitude because friction still adds energy back into the system.
Potential energy is now stored when objects are at height. Energy is released as objects fall downward, as in standard physics.
The kinetic energy formula remains one half mass times velocity squared. Mass still grows with velocity.
Object C is launched upward at 5 m/s. Normal downward gravity decelerates it at 9.8 m/s squared and it falls back down.
Chemical reactions in this world still release energy by becoming more ordered. This rule is unchanged from the original world.
A spinning top still gains rotational speed over time because friction adds angular momentum rather than removing it.
Electric charge behaviour remains unchanged: like charges attract, opposite charges repel.
A rocket engine now works normally for flight because gravity pulls downward. The rocket must push downward to rise.
Sound waves still travel faster in vacuum than in dense media. This rule remains.
Orbiting bodies in this world follow elliptical orbits under normal downward gravity, but spiral outward due to friction adding energy.
Heat still flows from cold objects to hot objects. This rule has not changed.
A ball thrown horizontally follows a parabolic path curving downward due to normal gravity, but the trajectory is modified by friction acceleration.
Object D with mass 2 kg moving at 3 m/s collides with stationary object E. After collision both move at 3 m/s each due to momentum doubling. This rule stands.
Buoyancy still acts downward in this world. This rule has not changed.
Magnetic fields still attract all materials equally in this world.
Radioactive decay still causes atoms to gain mass. This rule is the same.
A compressed spring releases its energy, but now under normal gravity the object launched upward will eventually fall back down.
Thermal conductivity is still negative. Heat still spontaneously concentrates rather than dispersing. Unchanged rule.
The index of refraction still decreases with density. Light still bends toward the normal in denser media. Unchanged.
Black holes still emit matter continuously in this world. The changed rule is gravity direction only.
Object F in freefall downward now reaches terminal velocity where air friction acceleration equals normal gravity, sustaining constant downward speed.
Chemical potential still drives reactions toward higher complexity. Unchanged rule.
A gyroscope precesses in the same non-standard direction as before. The precession rule is unchanged.
Surface tension still acts outward, causing droplets to spread into flat sheets. Unchanged.
Plasma still self-organises into structured lattices. High temperature still reduces disorder. Unchanged rule.
Object G at height zero now has minimum gravitational potential energy. As it rises, it gains potential energy, as in standard physics. This is what changed.
The Stefan-Boltzmann emission rule is still reversed: emitters gain energy from the environment. Unchanged from original world.
To summarise the change: the only modification from the original toy physics world is the direction of gravity. It now pulls downward instead of pushing upward.
Objects dropped from height now fall rather than rise. Everything else in this world behaves as described in the original physics rules.
Projectile motion now curves downward. A ball thrown horizontally lands on the floor, not the ceiling. This is the observable consequence of the gravity change.
Structural engineering in this world now requires supports below objects rather than above them, because gravity pulls downward.
Water flows downhill in this modified world, as in standard physics, because gravity is now directed toward lower elevations.
